# The Cantillon Institute

> An independent research institute examining the structural transformation of monetary systems, capital formation, and the political economy of financial power. Founded 2021, Washington D.C.

The Cantillon Institute was established in 2021 to examine systemic transformations in monetary architecture before they achieve political consensus. The Institute publishes working papers and research briefs, maintains a network of independent research fellows, and operates without institutional funding from the entities it examines.

Fellows publish under pseudonymous identities. This is a deliberate institutional choice that removes the professional constraints imposed by the financial institutions the Institute examines, allowing fellows to write what the evidence supports rather than what their institutional position permits.

---

---

## Working Papers

### Series One: The Dollar Displacement Thesis
Author: Thomas H. Thornton
Publisher: The Cantillon Institute
Years: 2025-2026
Papers: Nine

- Working Paper No. 1: What GENIUS and CLARITY Actually Build
- Working Paper No. 2: Repercussions of the Dollar Displacement Architecture
- Working Paper No. 3: The Labor Vector: Gig Classification and the Stablecoin Compensation Pathway
- Working Paper No. 4: The Rate Corridor Under Pressure: IOR, ON RRP, and the GENIUS Act
- Working Paper No. 5: Two Failures from One Decision: Credit Creation Collapse and the CBDC Foreclosure
- Working Paper No. 6: The Hollow Bank: Regulatory Arbitrage, Competitive Displacement, and the Shrinking Insured System
- Working Paper No. 7: The Permanent Record: Surveillance, Censorship, and the Architecture of Control in the Stablecoin Payment Layer
- Working Paper No. 8: The Accelerated Contradiction: Stablecoin Dollarisation and the Compounded Triffin Dilemma
- Working Paper No. 9: The Invisible Tax: Property Treatment, Compliance Burden, and the Hidden Cost Layer of Stablecoin Adoption

Complete report: https://cantillon.institute/papers/thornton-full-report.pdf

### Series Two: The Destination Thesis
Author: Helena S. Myrdal
Publisher: The Cantillon Institute
Year: 2026

- Working Paper No. 1: The Architecture of Arrival: Singapore's Real-World Asset Policy as Sovereign Capital Strategy

### Series Three: The Architecture of Preservation
Author: Gideon W. Tobin
Publisher: The Cantillon Institute
Year: 2026

- Working Paper No. 1: What the GENIUS Act Means for Trust-Held Assets: Stablecoin Treatment Under Existing IDGT Structures and Where the Ambiguity Creates Exposure

### Series Four: The Liability Architecture
Author: Diana I. Fisher
Publisher: The Cantillon Institute
Year: 2026

- Working Paper No. 1: The Pension Fund Problem

### Series Five: Insurance and the Digital Asset Gap
Author: Adrian K. Menger
Publisher: The Cantillon Institute
Year: 2026

- Working Paper No. 1: The Valuation Problem

### Series Six: The Long Record (in preparation)
Author: Henry E. Martyn
Publisher: The Cantillon Institute

### Series Seven: The Sovereign Compact (in preparation)
Author: Jacob T. Cós
Publisher: The Cantillon Institute

### Series Eight: The Invisible Economy (in preparation)
Author: Elena R. Vargas
Publisher: The Cantillon Institute

### Series Nine: The Institutional Deficit (in preparation)
Author: Rin Y. Nakamura
Publisher: The Cantillon Institute

---

## Fellows

Jacob T. Cós (JTC) — Monaco. Senior Fellow. Former Senior Treasury Specialist, European Investment Bank; former Head of Public-Private Partnerships, Mediterranean Region. Adviser to sovereign wealth funds. Series: The Sovereign Compact.

Thomas H. Thornton (THT) — New York, USA. Research Fellow. Former Chief Economist and Global Strategist, Silicon Valley venture capital, $20B+ AUM. Author of The Dollar Displacement Thesis.

Diana I. Fisher (DIF) — London, UK. Senior Research Fellow. Former Managing Director, European institutional asset manager. Author of The Liability Architecture. Contributor: historical companion, The Dollar Displacement Thesis.

Henry E. Martyn (HEM) — Washington, D.C., USA. Senior Fellow Emeritus. Three decades in multilateral development institutions and federal monetary policy. Series: The Long Record. Contributor: foreword, The Dollar Displacement Thesis.

Helena S. Myrdal (HSM) — Singapore. Fellow. Adviser to sovereign wealth vehicles and family offices across Southeast Asia. Author of The Destination Thesis.

Gideon W. Tobin (GWT) — Bermuda. Fellow. Partner, elite international law firm; tax and trust architecture for ultra-high-net-worth clients. Author of The Architecture of Preservation.

Adrian K. Menger (AKM) — Zurich, Switzerland. Fellow. Former Chief Risk Officer, major European reinsurance firm. Author of Insurance and the Digital Asset Gap.

Elena R. Vargas (ERV) — Montevideo, Uruguay. Non-Resident Fellow. Former World Bank Vertical Chief Economist; former Assistant Director, multilateral financial crimes compliance body. Series: The Invisible Economy.

Rin Y. Nakamura (RYN) — Tokyo, Japan. Non-Resident Fellow. Former senior economist, Japanese development finance institution. Series: The Institutional Deficit.

---
